descriptions: After the conflict, Tín Mã Nàm negotiated and ceded territory to Đại Cathay.
edges.evidence_quote: Tín Mã Nàm was then forced to call for a negotiation with Đại, where he and many of the Chinese gangs in Chợ Lớn decided to give up away the areas between Nancy market and District 1 to Đại Cathay's gang.

descriptions: After negotiation, Tín Mã Nàm's gang ceded territory to Đại Cathay's gang, indicating a cooperative settlement.

Grendon crime family.description: Irish organised crime syndicate based in Ireland, emerging in the late 20th century. Concentrated within Ireland, with a hierarchical family-led structure.

'Ndrina Gallelli.description: The 'Ndrina Gallelli is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta criminal organization based in Badolato, Calabria, Italy. It is involved in extortion, illegal land guardianship, and fraudulently obtaining European Union funds. The clan has been active since at least the 1970s and has faced multiple police operations, including Itaca-Freeboat in 2013 and Pietranera in 2017, leading to arrests and convictions.

Frankfurt mafia.description: The Frankfurt mafia is a Macedonian crime group founded in the mid-1990s in Veles, North Macedonia, involved in heroin trafficking in Frankfurt, Germany, and Vienna, Austria. It controlled about 90% of the drug trade in Frankfurt and 19 out of 24 blocks in Vienna's drug trade. Despite large-scale arrests in 2010 and 2013, the group remains active and has changed tactics.

Originated in the California Youth Authority in the 1970s, created by Aryan Brotherhood members. Known for extreme violence and involvement in drug trafficking, extortion, and murder. Allied with Aryan Brotherhood, Mexican Mafia, and other gangs. Active both in and outside prison.

Charlton Street Gang.description: The Charlton Street Gang was a group of river pirates active on the Hudson River in New York City during the mid-19th century. They raided small cargo ships in New York Harbor and later moved upriver, stealing a sloop in 1869 under the leadership of Sadie the Goat. The gang engaged in kidnapping for ransom and was known for flying the Jolly Roger. They dissolved by the end of the 1860s after conflicts with vigilantes.

Cartel del Norte del Valle.description: Colombian drug trafficking organization based in the northern Valle del Cauca region, active from the 1980s to 2008. It emerged after the fragmentation of the Cali and Medellín cartels in the mid-1990s, becoming one of the most powerful cocaine trafficking groups. Known for extreme violence and internal betrayals, it was led by Orlando Henao Montoya until his death in 1998, after which it split into factions. The organization was dismantled by 2008, though small dissident groups remain active.

Spook Hunters.description: White racist youth gang based in South Gate, California, active from the 1940s to the 1950s. Founded as a reaction to the growing African American population in Los Angeles, the group opposed integration and racial segregation. They instigated fights and intimidated black youths, prompting the formation of protective black gangs like the Slausons.

Bonanno crime family.description: The Bonanno crime family is one of the Five Families of the American Mafia in New York City, founded by Salvatore Maranzano in the 1890s. It has historically been the smallest and most brutal of the Five Families, with operations extending into New Jersey, Florida, California, Arizona, and Montreal. The family was involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, and labor union corruption, and was known for internal conflicts such as the Banana War in the 1960s. It remains active as of the 2020s.

Arifs.description: The Arifs are a Turkish Cypriot criminal organization based in south-east London, involved in armed robbery, drug trafficking, and racketeering since the late 1960s. They rose to prominence after the Kray brothers' downfall and were considered Britain's leading crime family in the late 1980s. The gang was led by brothers Deniz, Mehmet, and Doğan Arif, but many members were convicted in the 1990s. They maintain ties with relatives in Turkey for drug shipments.

edges.evidence_quote: Following the downfall of the Kray brothers , the Arifs were one of several criminal organisations who took control of the London underworld including the Clerkenwell crime syndicate and the Brindle family. with whom they were engaged in a highly publicised gangland war during the 1990s.
